videos.title: Biking the Big Apple: Exploring NYC's Five Boro Bike Tour
videos.description:
My second bike event of the year was right here in NYC!
Watch this video to pick up some tips and find out what I thought of the ride.

Register here: https://www.bike.nyc/events/td-five-boro-bike-tour/
videos.transcript:
[Music]
hey everybody it's Jan and I'm back with
a another review video of another bike
race that I did earlier this year this
one is super overdue I did this back in
May it was the five bar bike tour here
in New York so like the last videos I'm
going to share my four dislikes and my
four likes of the five barel Bike Tour
and share a little background of what my
experience was like doing this race for
the first time so before I get into the
likes and the dislikes some of the tips
that I got about doing the Race I should
start as early as possible because
there's a lot of people so try to get
close to the front and making sure that
the bike was in good condition and also
just general tip to have fun so it
seemed pretty easy going into it I ended
up doing this with a friend of mine
because I had a guest pass for the race
so they joined me for the first time as
well now I normally bike around New York
so I'm pretty comfortable riding on the
streets in the bike Lanes on bike paths
but the five burrow is unique in the
sense that people who don't typically
ride in the streets on the day-to-day
get a chance to ride carfree and they
get a chance to go through all five
barels hence the name five barel Bike
Tour so it is probably one of the only
times that I ever actually bike through
all five burrows but I would say in
general I'm pretty comfortable biking on
the streets any other day now this event
was huge it was a ton of people there
were several different waves that you
sign up for depending on the time your
experience level oh another thing that I
was advised to do was as soon as you
finish and pass the Finish Line Get Your
medal maybe take a picture or two and
like book it straight for the fairy and
that's pretty much what we did and it
worked out great for us so I would
advise for anyone else who decides to do
this to not stay long when you finish
the race at Staten Island is to get in
the fairy line as quickly as possible
because the line does get long the
fairies are still on a schedule they
come every so often and they fill up
pretty fast especially when you have a
bunch of people with bikes all trying to
get on the boat at the same time so
let's get into the likes and the
dislikes and you know what I'm going to
do start with the dislikes so we can end
on a high note with the likes all right
so dislike number one the
route so I personally was not a huge fan
of the route and while yes it does go
through all five burrows I just feel
like New York has so many amazing
Skyline viewpoints and just sites and
things to see iconic buildings and what
have you that this route didn't
necessarily cover all of those things so
I feel like if you're expecting the five
burrow to take you past all the most
iconic sites in New York is not going to
do that like this route is strictly just
so you are able to say you biked through
all Five Barrels which was a little
disappointing especially coming off of
the Cape Town ride because that
one was actually just more of a race but
it took me through so much of Cape Town
like I felt like I got a chance to see
everything and I understand with New
York and space restraints and with just
how many people signed up for the event
that was probably a bit challenging to
do but yeah the route wasn't that
impressive to me in the sense of things
to see but I could be a little harsh on
this because I live here and maybe for
someone who is coming in from out of
town or just doing this for the first
time or never bikes in the city
ever maybe for them it's a highlight and
they were completely unfazed by it all
right number two there were a lot of
potholes now I wasn't surprised by that
because I'm aware that there's tons of
potholes here in New York but when
you're on a bike especially in some of
these streets that I don't typically
bike on uh especially as you get to the
BQE and different sections like that
where it's really only for cars those
areas I was really grateful that I made
sure there was enough air in my tires
and my bike was in pretty good condition
because yeah you hit some pretty hard
potholes number three the verzano
so something that everyone told me that
was going to be a highlight was getting
a chance to finally bike across the
verzano bridge and if you don't know the
verzano bridge is one of the few Bridges
here in New York that does not have a
bike path or pedestrian path I believe
that you're able to bike across it and
so the five barel Bike Tour is that one
time a year really that you get a chance
to actually bike across this iconic
Bridge so I was pretty excited about
this part but the reason why it was on
my dislikes list is because when you're
riding on the verzano you're riding on
the lower level of traffic which means
you're not riding under those beautiful
arches you're not getting unobstructed
views of the water some people people
decided to pull over to the side while
they were on the verzano and try to get
a picture that way or to just take a
break for me I just kind of wanted to
knock it out since I was going uphill
most of the way but yeah that was kind
of a let down to not be able to go over
the top and go under the arches of the
bridge and really get a chance to get
that full
experience but I'm guessing it's
probably a lot of logistics that go into
putting together the five barrel and
maybe what it takes to shut down the top
level is maybe more complicated than
just allowing the cyclist to go on the
lower level but yeah that was kind of a
let down so I wasn't too crazy about
that part it kind of just felt like an
unnecessary uphill on the last stretch
of the entire route to me okay and then
number four of my dislikes on this list
is that the end wasn't n really the end
I realized when I went through the
Finish Line I was short about 2 miles or
so and I was confused because I thought
that as Advertiser was going to hit that
exact mile marker that I signed up for
and so I was wondering why I was off by
so much I thought there was something
wrong with my bike computer that it just
didn't record the first couple of miles
but what I found out was the finish line
is a few miles
before the actual end which is where you
need to bike to to get to the ferry so
once my friend and I got back on our
bikes and started heading towards the
ferry as we were advised then that's
where I noticed that I picked up the
couple last couple of miles that I
needed to get the full total so if they
end up keeping the route the same I just
would flag that for those people who
really want to get their m in and want
to get that number recorded as the
number that they signed up for don't
turn your bike computer off just yet
when you go through the Finish Line
because you're not done if you keep
riding a couple miles to the ferry then
you get the full amount that is
advertised okay so those were my
dislikes pretty quick four dislikes for
the five burrow now we can get into the
likes okay so my number one like of the
five barow Bike Tour is that it was
really well organized now despite
everything I just said in the dislike
section I will say overall the whole
event was extremely organized like from
the emails that were sent out beforehand
to the packet pickup was extremely
organized we showed up to the packet
pickup location and there was a line
wrapped around the block completely like
the beginning of the line met the end of
the line if that makes sense it went all
the way around a city a full city block
and it took us no time to get through
there and get our stuff it was super
super super organized shout out to bike
New York for making sure that it was a
seamless process we were able to get
everything that we needed
beforehand and while biking along the
Route it had plenty of support and the
rest stops were organized like it was
it's just a really well executed event
so that is definite going to be on the
likes list for me number two I'm going
to say it I'm going to bring it up as I
do in pretty much every video now the
snacks so this event has the snacks on
the likes list because they had a lot of
great options honestly I would say for
this one especially since it's less than
50 Mi you don't even really need to pack
any snacks definitely bring water but
there's so many snacks at all of the
rest stops that you really don't need to
worry about packing a bunch for yourself
to start the ride I would just eat a
good breakfast before you start and you
can pretty much just rely on what's
provided okay so number three this was
good for all levels or let's say most
levels I'm not sure really little kids
can maybe handle this but I would say
for people who generally like to buy C
usually maybe not all the
time would be able to do this
successfully and enjoy it I know some
people who they don't bike for like the
whole year and then in May when the five
barrel rolls around this is the first
thing they do it's more of a ride than a
race you're going at a much slower Pace
just simply because there are so many
people so you don't feel this pressure
to keep up or to ride really fast or to
overexert yourself you're definitely
going at pretty much a conversational
pace which makes it really easy to
tackle whatever little Hills do come up
and to be able to just enjoy the ride
this is great for people who are just
getting into longdistance rides and so I
would say it's good for all different
levels which makes it way more inclusive
and I think if anyone is interested in
doing it while they're here in New York
in May
you definitely should because it's not
too challenging and it's still pretty
fun okay Number Four The Finisher medal
now I have told everyone this before and
I'm going to say it here I think all of
the bike New York events have the best
finisher Medals of any of the bike
events that I've been to so far these
medals are so well done they're really
creative they're really Hefty so it
feels like a real win like a real
accomplishment this was the 45th five
burrow that they held so the metal was
in the shape of a
45 if I have it I'll share a picture it
was definitely the coolest metal that I
think I received all year or pretty much
since I've been doing these whoever is
making their
medals deserves a race because these I'm
telling you are really great and if
you're really into receiving medals when
you do these types of events or races
you're definitely going to enjoy one
from a bike New York event or from the
five burrow itself if you choose to do
it every year or just for the first time
I would say I'm really impressed by the
hardware that you get when you finally
finish one of these events okay so
that's it that's my review of the five
burrow I would say overall it went
pretty well I think it's great though
for people who are nearby and want to
attempt the route and for those who like
I said because it is good for all levels
I think for people who usually too
nervous to get out there and ride any
other day in New York cuz I get it the
streets are crazy and between the cabs
and the other cyclists and the buses and
people and scooters and all the things
out on the streets it can be really
intimidating to get out there but but I
think the five barel is a good
introduction to biking along the streets
and you don't have to worry about cars
and you don't have to go super fast if
you don't want to and there are snacks
and like I said it's really well
supported and well organized so I would
recommend it especially if you're just
getting started on these types of major
bike events and you're in New York or
just the New York area there were
definitely people from out of town that
signed up it's a pretty big deal not as
big of a deal as the New York City
marathon for those who run but I will
say it's a pretty good turnout and
people really do enjoy it so I would
recommend I just don't think that it's
really something that I'm going to
do again very soon so I think that's it
for me thank you guys so much for
watching another one of these videos if
you haven't seen some of my other ones
check out the Cape Town trip that I did
earlier this year in
March you can also check out the one
that I recently did in Denver in
September and hopefully we have one more
coming up to end the year in the fourth
quarter in November so check back for
that one later thank you for watching I
hope this was helpful and I'll see you
in the next
one

videos.title: Five Boro Bike Tour brings cyclists from around the world to NYC
videos.description: Organizers said it's not a race, but a chance to ride together and take in the beautiful sights of the city, CBS2's Christina Fan reports.
videos.transcript:
it was a perfect day for a bike ride and
thousands of people did just that across
every Borough CBS 2's Christina fan
takes us to the annual five Borough Bike
Tour and explains the inspiration behind
the event
40 miles five boroughs zero cars and one
beautiful spring day
[Applause]
32 000 enthusiastic cyclists embarked
from Lower Manhattan Sunday morning
pedaling through the Bronx Queens
Brooklyn before wrapping up on Staten
Island it's awesome the energy the
people it's a really cool Community
first timers and Old-Timers experienced
Riders and newbies took in the sights of
the city together I like the idea that
there's no cars because it's usually a
little scary to ride on the streets here
I love it you know I I heard about it I
said you know New York City is the place
to go and try it why not so I'm coming
from Toronto Canada registration for
this event doesn't just get you a ride
it also helps support charity work by
bike New York we provide over 30 000 New
Yorkers with free education we teach
them how to ride how to ride safely
we're also training former incarcerated
New Yorkers to become Pike mechanics
then City bikes hiring them this year
marked the five Borough bike tours 45th
year and Adele sawana is the millionth
Rider I think it's a really good way to
like these things see in New York like
in the larger space and I've never even
been to Staten Island so it's really my
first like first of many there's no
other ride in the city like this and no
other day where the streets are this car
free and Lower Manhattan Christina fan
CBS 2 News
